metadata.summary: Provision AWS ALB/NLB and GCP Global HTTP(S) Load Balancers with Terraform: target groups, health checks, TLS listeners, S3 access logs, deletion protection, and WAF integration.

content.title: Cloud Load Balancer Provisioning with Terraform
content.summary: Terraform modules for AWS ALB (Application Load Balancer) and NLB (Network Load Balancer), and GCP Global HTTP(S) Load Balancer. Covers: aws_lb resource with deletion protection and access logs to S3, target groups with health check configuration, HTTPS listener with ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06, HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect listener, security groups, and GCP backend services with CDN policy, URL maps, and managed SSL certificates.

why.p: Cloud managed LBs (AWS ALB/NLB, GCP Cloud LB) eliminate the operational overhead of HA, certificate renewal, and failover that self-hosted HAProxy/Nginx require. Terraform is the standard IaC vehicle because it provides: state management for drift detection, plan/apply workflow for safe changes, and modular reuse across environments. AWS ALB and GCP Global LB are anycast-aware and integrate natively with WAF, Shield, and Cloud Armor.

list.item: Provisioning a new cloud LB for a service on AWS or GCP using Terraform.
Migrating from self-hosted HAProxy/Nginx to AWS ALB or GCP Cloud LB.
Adding WAF, Shield, or Cloud Armor to an existing cloud LB via Terraform.
Setting up NLB for TCP/UDP workloads (databases, Redis) on AWS.
Kubernetes workloads — use the AWS Load Balancer Controller or GKE Ingress instead of raw aws_lb resources; the controllers manage target group registration automatically.
Self-hosted bare-metal environments — Terraform AWS/GCP providers require cloud API access; use HAProxy plus keepalived instead.
Azure — use azurerm_application_gateway or azurerm_frontdoor; the patterns differ from AWS/GCP.

example.title: Terraform: complete AWS ALB with HTTPS, target group, redirect, security group, S3 logs
Terraform: AWS NLB for TCP/UDP workloads
example.note: Use NLB (not ALB) for TCP/UDP protocols. NLB does not support path-based routing or WAF; use ALB for HTTP(S).

example.title: Terraform: GCP Global HTTP(S) Load Balancer with CDN and URL map

section.name: llm-prompts-cloud
section.prompt:
Generate Terraform configuration for AWS Application Load Balancer:
Requirements: - Environment: [production/staging] - VPC ID: [VPC_ID] - Subnets: [SUBNET_IDS] - Certificate ARN: [CERT_ARN]
Target groups: - web: port 8080, health check /health - api: port 3000, health check /health/ready
Features: - Access logs to S3 - Deletion protection - HTTP to HTTPS redirect - WAF integration (optional)
Include security groups and outputs.

Plan migration from [HAProxy/Nginx] to [AWS ALB/GCP LB/Azure LB]:
Current configuration: [PASTE CONFIG]
Current features used: - [LIST FEATURES]
Requirements: - Zero downtime migration - Feature parity where possible - Cost optimization
Provide: 1. Feature mapping (current -> cloud equivalent) 2. Migration steps 3. Terraform configuration for new setup 4. Rollback plan 5. Validation checklist

rule.statement: AWS ALB MUST have enable_deletion_protection = true and access_logs block pointing to an S3 bucket in all non-ephemeral environments. The S3 bucket MUST have a lifecycle rule expiring logs after 90 days minimum.
rule.rationale: ALB deletion protection prevents accidental terraform destroy from taking down production traffic. Access logs to S3 are the audit trail for compliance (PCI-DSS, HIPAA) and the raw data for post-incident traffic analysis. Without them, debugging a past incident is impossible.

rule.statement: AWS ALB HTTPS listener MUST use ssl_policy = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06" or a more recent policy. Never use "ELBSecurityPolicy-2016-08" or any policy that includes TLS 1.0 or 1.1.
rule.rationale: AWS updates SSL policies; older policy names remain valid but include deprecated ciphers and TLS versions. The 2021-06 policy enforces TLS 1.2 minimum with TLS 1.3 preferred and excludes all known-weak ciphers.

checklist.check: Load balancer type correct (ALB for HTTP/HTTPS, NLB for TCP/UDP).
Security groups allow only required traffic.
Access logs enabled to S3 with lifecycle expiry.
Health check path correct; interval and thresholds set.
Deregistration delay configured (30s default; increase for long-lived connections).
HTTPS listener with valid certificate ARN.
HTTP to HTTPS redirect listener configured.
SSL policy is ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06 or newer.
VPC endpoint for private access if applicable.

section.name: cloud-lb-quota-gotchas
section.p: Cloud LB quota limits that agents silently hit at scale:

list.item: AWS ALB: 100 target groups per LB by default; 100 rules per listener. Request limit increases before reaching 80%.
AWS NLB: 200 targets per target group for instance type; 500 for IP type.
GCP: 50 backend services per URL map; 100 forwarding rules per project.
AWS: ALB quotas are per-region; multi-region deployments each have their own limits.
